behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local lint-rule logic over template text; no module handoff, OS/network/subprocess, or model output is required. Rejection gate: higher levels are disqualified by behavior text 'static prompt-template lint check' and 'prompt-template' (pure local analysis).
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative prompt-template strings (including copied phase instructions and near-miss benign text) into the lint function and assert reject/accept decisions plus exact violation spans.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module boundaries: template + interpolator + fixture injection + assembler. Unit is too low because leakage can be introduced during handoff. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement, no dependence on model output quality, no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt assembly pipeline with production-like fixtures and interpolation inputs, then lint the final assembled prompt for leaked phase instructions.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on model behavior being driven by loaded skill context, not deterministic wrapper plumbing alone. A mock model could pass while hard-coded prompt rules still dominate, hiding the real failure. Rejection gate: Integration is disqualified by 'validates behavior from that context' (model decision/content dependence).
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real invocation with live model and real skill file loading, then verify output behavior consistent with skill-context guidance while ensuring prompt text itself does not contain duplicated phase rules.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: This is harness wiring/state selection across prompt input + skill-file variant resolution; deterministic stubs can catch it. Integration-brake: no required real subprocess/network; model output quality is not asserted here; no multi-step agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Parameterize harness to point at normal vs degraded/missing SKILL.md fixtures while keeping user prompt constant, and assert invocation succeeds in both modes with correct variant selection metadata.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly tests behavior regression from changed skill content under identical prompt; success/failure depends on real model output differences. Mocking the model would hide the failure mode. Rejection gate: Integration disqualified by behavior text 'behavioral test fails' tied to degraded SKILL.md content.
behaviors.test_description: Run A/B live-model executions with identical user prompt against baseline skill vs degraded skill variant; assert structural behavioral expectations pass on baseline and fail on degraded variant.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears in ordering across components: model-call wrapper, artifact writer, parser/extractor. Unit may miss sequencing bugs across modules. Integration-brake: local filesystem artifact is sufficient; no real OS/subprocess/network contract required beyond in-process wiring for this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the pipeline with a controlled raw response and assert artifact file/write event occurs before parser invocation (e.g., timestamp/order hook), and artifact preserves unmodified raw text.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: This is assertion-engine logic over given outputs; one local rule set can be tested without I/O or model calls. Rejection gate: higher levels disqualified because behavior text targets assertion semantics themselves, not infrastructure or live model variability.
behaviors.test_description: Feed crafted outputs that pass keyword checks but violate schema path/role placement into assertion functions; verify structural assertions fail and valid structured outputs pass.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires live model execution and regression detection based on real output variation/quality. Deterministic mocks can’t prove this miss-classification gap. Rejection gate: Integration disqualified by 'Under live model execution' and comparison against real regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model tests on prompts known to produce plausible keyword-containing but structurally wrong outputs after skill degradation; assert keyword-only check would pass while structural assertions fail.
